EGFR: Epidermal growth factor receptor — a gene often mutated in lung cancer; target for TKIs like osimertinib.
osimertinib: A third-generation EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor (Tagrisso), commonly first-line for EGFR-mutant NSCLC.
LVEF: Left ventricular ejection fraction — heart pump strength measured by echocardiogram; many trials require LVEF ≥ 50%.
ECOG: Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status (0–5); lower scores mean the patient is more active.
NSCLC: Non-small cell lung cancer — the most common type of lung cancer.
TKI: Tyrosine kinase inhibitor — a targeted therapy that blocks cancer growth signals.
RECIST: Response Evaluation Criteria in Solid Tumors — standard rules for measuring tumor size on scans.
HER2: Human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 — a protein that promotes cancer cell growth; target in breast cancer trials.
